-- 1 of 5 --

Building Your List
!
The number one core strategy I want to cover here. This would be my number one core
strategy no matter what product, what I was teaching you in terms of business, and
that is the list.
!
Always be focused on your list and building your list.
The reality is the wealth is in the list. For most businesses the wealth is in the list. It’s
the list of prospects and the list of clients or customers.
!
I stumbled onto this early. There were two things I stumbled onto early and this was
one of them. I started literally building my list before I even had a website. That’s how I
stumbled on it early. And I’ve been focused on list building since 1996, so I’ve gotten
pretty good at it.
!
You always want to be building your list, you always want to be focusing on your list,
you always want to be focused on your list building process. You want to develop an
obsession with list building.
!
Now, no one starts out with a list.
!
That’s really important. I wasn’t born with a list. I didn’t come out of the womb with a
list.
!
I have built significant lists in multiple different markets. I’ve built tens of thousands of
person lists in several different markets.
No one starts with a list, but the successful people start building their list early and
often. No one starts with a list, but the smart folks start building that list.
!
One thing to remember is a lot of people obsess that they have to have a big list. They
think, “It’s not going to do any good until I have 1,000 people,” or 10,000 people, or
30,000 people, or 100,000 people. Get that out of your mind. Everyone starts with zero
subscribers and they build it from there.
!
The thing is that small lists are strong. Your results, your conversions from the first
1,000 people are going to be strong. I’ve seen many times in launches that small lists
outperform much bigger lists.
!
It isn’t like a 1,000 person list is going to outperform a 100,000 person list. But a 1,000
person list on a per subscriber basis is generally going to get much better results than
that 100,000 person list. This is just a universal law of the universe.
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 2 of 5 --

The basics of list building are simple. This is why I include a list building course within
Product Launch Formula, because this is so critical and so important.
!
You need three things:
! 1. 	A squeeze page
2. 	An opt-in bribe - what we call an ethical bribe
3. 	Traffic - you need to put some kind of traffic to that squeeze page.
!
The squeeze page with the ethical bribe is like, “Join my list and I’ll give you this thing,”
so you need to create the page that makes that promise and then you drive traffic to
that page. Boom, when people opt-in to get their bribe, they’re building your list.
!
I’m not going to go into this deep right now because I’m giving you this entire
Launching Your List 2.0 course with Product Launch Formula and we’re going to go
plenty deep there. The thing is always focus on building your list.
!
Launches are a great way for building your list.
I’ve had launches where I have put 30,000 to 50,000 people on my list in a matter of
10 days. That’s awesome and incredible. That’s with a joint venture launch (JV Launch)
where you have lots of other people with big lists mailing into your squeeze page,
mailing into your first piece of prelaunch content, mailing to your second piece of
prelaunch content and third piece of prelaunch content. That’s really awesome.
!
Joint venture launches are great ways to build your list. However, they’re not the only
way launches build your list.
!
We’ve seen this over and over where a well run, well orchestrated, well engineered
internal launch, or even a seed launch, you put out that good content and word starts
to go around and just word of mouth you can build your list.
!
We’ve seen this over and over where people maybe have a 1,000 or 2,000 person list
and they might add 1,000 people onto their list during their launch even without joint
venture partners.
!
This happens more nowadays because of social media. Social media makes it so easy to
share your prelaunch content, so people will share your prelaunch content and the
people they share it with will generally come into your squeeze page and opt-in to get
your prelaunch content. So even outside of joint venture launches, even just internal
launches or seed launches, you can build your list.
Launches are awesome for list building, but remember you should always be
building your list, including when you’re not in a launch.
!
Slow and steady wins the race.

-- 3 of 5 --

!
When I started publishing about the stock market, initially I got one subscriber every
three or four days. Eventually I worked up and I was getting one subscriber a day, and
that was really exciting. Then I worked and there was sort of a threshold around 3 or 4
subscribers I would average per day. That was pretty cool because that meant I was
getting about 100 people a month who were joining my list and I was really pretty
amped about that.
!
I kept working on it. I worked on bringing in more traffic, I worked on my opt-in
process, worked on the squeeze page, worked on the bribe, on and on. Eventually I got
it up to around 30 people per day. That’s almost 1,000 people per month. That ends up
being 10,000 to 12,000 people per year.
!
I just kept on polishing that thing for years and I eventually built that list up to about
80,000 subscribers. That’s the one that started with 17 subscribers and getting one new
subscriber every three or four days. Through optimizing that process and moving
forward, I built that up into a serious list.
!
Here’s the most important thing about lists... the actual list and the size of the list is not
very important. It’s your relationship to the list.
!
Do they open your emails? Do they do whatever you tell them to do? Do they click on
your links?
!
I have all kinds of insight from being on the insides of many launches, including big
joint venture launches, and I’ve seen this over and over where people have huge lists
that don’t perform and other people have small lists that do perform. It’s all about that
list relationship. Again, I’m going to go way deep on this in the Launching Your List
course, but relationship trumps list size.
!
The other thing is, and this is sort of counterintuitive, the best relationship comes from
doing a launch.
!
A lot of times people think, “If I do a launch and I send people a whole bunch of
prelaunch content, all this email about my launch, they’re going to hate me.”
!
The reality is if you’re putting out good content and you have a good sideways sales
letter, and that sideways sales letter leads people through this sequence of opportunity,
transformation, ownership experience, and really teaches and shares throughout that
experience and tells a good story over that whole launch story arc, then people are
going to love you.
!
When you have the absolute warmest list is after the launch. The day after the launch
ends is when you have the warmest list. Both the people who buy from you and the
people who haven’t bought from you that are just still on your prospect list, they’re
going to love you.

-- 4 of 5 --

Launches are a great way to build that launch relationship.
!
Remember, no one starts off with a list. I didn’t start off with a list. When I changed
markets – and I’ve changed markets several times – I didn’t have a list, each time I just
started over and started building that list. This is a case of baby steps.
